rules.summary: Unless otherwise stated, this Part applies to a non-directive insurer which carries on long-term insurance business , other than a non-directive friendly society.
Except for 16.3(1)") this Part does not apply to final bonuses.
In this Part, the following definitions shall apply:
1. (1) Subject to (2), a firm must establish its mathematical reserves using a prospective actuarial valuation on prudent assumptions of all future cash flows expected to arise under, or in respec
In the actuarial valuation under 2.1, a firm must use methods and prudent assumptions which:
1. (1) Subject to (2) and (3), a firm must determine the amount of the mathematical reserves separately for each contract of long-term insurance.
A firm may calculate a negative value for the mathematical reserves in respect of a contract of long-term insurance provided that:
1. (1) A firm must establish mathematical reserves for a contract of insurance which are sufficient to ensure that, at any subsequent date, the mathematical reserves then required are covered
In a prospective valuation, a firm must:

The risk-adjusted yield is either:
The risk-adjusted yield on a basket of assets is the arithmetic mean of the risk-adjusted yield on each asset weighted by that asset's market value.
The running yield :
For the purposes of 9.5")(2):
The internal rate of return on an asset is the annual rate of interest which, if used to calculate the present value of future income (before deduction of tax) and of repayments of capital (before d
In both the running yield and internal rate of return the yield must be reduced to exclude that part of the yield that represents compensation for credit risk arising from the asset.
Provision for credit risk for securities that are not credit-rated must be made on principles at least as prudent as those adopted for credit-rated securities.
The risk-adjusted yield assumed for the investment or reinvestment of sterling sums (other than sums expected to be received within the next three years) must not exceed the lowest of:
The risk-adjusted yield assumed for the investment or reinvestment of those sterling sums expected to be received within the next three years must not exceed the risk-adjusted yield on the assets
The risk-adjusted yield assumed for the investment or reinvestment of sums denominated in a currency other than sterling must be at least as prudent as in [10.1](/pra-rules/insurance-company-mathema

sections.chapter_title: Future Premiums: Adjustment for Deferred Acquisition Costs

rules.summary: 1. (1) The amount of any increase to the net premium for deferred acquisition costs must not exceed the equivalent of the recoverable acquisition expenses spread over the period of premium pay
This Chapter applies to accumulating with-profits policies written on a recurring single premium basis.
1. (1) A firm must not attribute any value to a future premium under the contract.
